narrative.headline: Florida is expanding both jobs and labor supply at the same time.
whatStandsOut: Nonfarm Payrolls are up 15.8k over the last year.
Labor Force is up 21,580 people year-over-year.
improving: Unemployment remains low by historical standards despite a 1.2 point annual increase.
Private Education & Health Services is among the strongest job growers (2.3% YoY).
Professional & Business Services is among the strongest job growers (0.8% YoY).
Leisure & Hospitality is among the strongest job growers (0.1% YoY).
whyItMatters: A growing labor force plus payroll expansion signals depth, not just short-term demand spikes.
Sector breadth helps Florida absorb national volatility while keeping opportunity distributed.
Population gains reinforce long-run demand for housing, services, and infrastructure investment.

narrative.headline: Florida’s innovation stack is expanding alongside its development engine.
signals: Professional & business services employment is up 0.8% year-over-year.
development: Real gross state product is up 11.9% over three years.
momentum: Innovation metrics are intended as directional signal layers, not standalone verdicts.
Business formation plus advanced-service employment growth is a strong expansion pattern.
Development capacity and innovation capacity should be tracked together for policy and capital allocation.

scorecard2030.title: The Florida Chamber scoreboard says the state is bigger, richer, and still short of its 2030 finish line.
scorecard2030.summary: This section brings the Florida Scorecard into the app as a real source. It ties the size of the current economy to the state's explicit 2030 targets instead of leaving the growth thesis as generic branding.

stats.label: Global economy rank
stats.context: If Florida were a country, based on a 2024 GDP baseline of $1.73T
stats.note: Inference: Florida's 2024 GDP baseline of $1.73T sits between Australia and Spain in the World Bank's 2024 current-dollar country table.

stats.label: 2030 jobs gap
stats.context: Net new jobs needed by 2030, State of Florida FutureCast

stats.label: Income migration
stats.context: Florida Chamber Foundation annual net income migration frame
stats.note: The Chamber's $36.05B annual figure annualizes to more than $4M per hour. The Scorecard currently shows a lower IRS-based hourly figure without a public note explaining the gap.

interpretation: The scorecard matters because it turns the Florida thesis into a scoreboard with explicit finish lines. Florida is already enormous, but the gap between rank 4 state GDP today and a number-one ambition by 2030 is still real.

The cleanest growth signal in this stack is not a vibe metric. It is business formation staying first nationally while the Chamber's public migration frame still clears four million dollars per hour into the state.

sources.label: Florida Scorecard, statewide innovation and economic development metrics
Florida Scorecard, State of Florida FutureCast
Florida Chamber Foundation income migration analysis
World Bank GDP current US dollars API

snowbirdIndex.title: Seasonal wealth migration is not a tourism story. This is a proxy until the Q4/Q1 snowbird series is wired.
snowbirdIndex.summary: This proxy build uses Florida Chamber migration metrics and retirement-county pull to show why the state keeps carrying housing, health care, and infrastructure pressure even when payroll growth softens.

stats.label: State income migration
stats.context: Florida Chamber Foundation annual net income migration frame
stats.note: The Chamber's $36.05B annual figure annualizes to more than $4M per hour. The Scorecard currently shows a lower IRS-based hourly figure without a public note explaining the gap.

stats.label: Collier pull
stats.context: Collier County income migration, Florida Scorecard

stats.label: Sumter pull
stats.context: Sumter County income migration, Florida Scorecard

stats.label: 2030 population range
stats.context: Statewide 2030 range, State of Florida FutureCast

interpretation: Florida's seasonal population story shows up as capital first. When Collier and Sumter pull that much income, the downstream pressure hits insurance, health care, housing supply, and local roads long before it shows up in a beach photo.
This is not a Miami-only story. The retirement counties help explain why statewide demand stays hot even when payroll momentum cools.

sources.label: Florida Chamber Foundation income migration analysis
Florida Scorecard, State of Florida FutureCast

spaceCoastCadence.title: Florida's innovation story is physical before it is financial.
spaceCoastCadence.summary: Launches, spaceport investment, and aerospace build-out are all rising in the same corridor. This is the part of the Florida innovation thesis that generic startup dashboards usually miss.

stats.label: Blue Origin expansion
stats.context: Cape Canaveral manufacturing project announced May 22, 2026

interpretation: The Space Coast matters because it turns aerospace from a branding asset into an operating system for manufacturing, dual-use, and infrastructure spending.
When launch cadence, project pipeline, and factory investment all rise together, Florida stops looking like a tourism economy with rockets on the side.

sources.label: Space Florida 2025 aerospace pipeline and launch update
Florida governor announcement on Blue Origin's Cape Canaveral expansion
Reuters on Blue Origin's Cape Canaveral expansion

latamGateway.title: Florida's gateway claim is physical, not rhetorical.
latamGateway.summary: The South Florida trade corridor is doing real freight work for Latin America and the Caribbean. This section brings PortMiami and Port Everglades into the trade story instead of treating the gateway line as a slogan.

stats.label: PortMiami tonnage
stats.context: FY2024 total tonnage, official PortMiami snapshot

stats.label: Port Everglades refrigerated cargo
stats.context: FY2024 refrigerated cargo leader in Florida

interpretation: PortMiami says almost half of its trade is tied to Latin America and the Caribbean, while Port Everglades is moving more than a million TEUs and leading Florida in refrigerated cargo. The gateway thesis is sitting on the dock, not in a keynote deck.
That matters because South Florida wins on adjacency. The same corridor handles containers, perishables, finance, and commercial relationships that keep Florida plugged into the Americas.

trade.headline: Florida Exports Hit $78.9B in 2025 - Largest Year in History
trade.releaseTitle: Florida Businesses Set Record High Export Sales in 2025

heroMetrics.label: Total Florida Exports (2025)
heroMetrics.helper: Largest export year in Florida history

heroMetrics.label: Bilateral Trade via FL Ports + Airports

deltas.label: Manufactured Export Share
deltas.baseLabel: 92% of all FL-origin exports are manufactured products

narrative.headline: Florida's export engine is firing on every cylinder - and 92% of it is built here.
whatStandsOut: $78.9B is the largest export year in Florida history - up $6.4B (+8.9%) vs 2024 and $21.6B (+37.8%) vs 2018.
Manufactured products = 92% of all Florida-origin exports ($72.2B). Florida's manufacturing sector is the fastest-growing in the country.
3 of Florida's top 5 export markets are in LATAM (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia) - reinforcing Florida as the Americas' commercial gateway.
Civilian aircraft and parts alone shipped $12.8B - aerospace is the single biggest export category.
watchOuts: 92% manufacturing concentration means the headline is sensitive to single-vertical shocks (aerospace cycle, pharma, telecom).
LATAM exposure (3 of top 5 markets) carries FX, political, and tariff-posture risk worth tracking quarter to quarter.
whyItMatters: $205B in bilateral trade through Florida seaports and airports reinforces the state's logistics-gateway position.
$1.9B in SelectFlorida-generated exports in FY24-25 (+$500M YoY) is direct, measurable ROI on Florida's trade-mission infrastructure.
A single trade show - Singapore Airshow Feb 2026 - drove $150M+ in reported sales for participating Florida companies.
